sections.paragraph:
αἴρω [Attic form = ἀείρω, etym. uncertain]

—1. ‘to cause to move upward’, raise up, lift: take up a pallet Mt 9:6; a cross 16:24; 27:32; pick up a stone J 8:59; 11:41. The 1st aor. act. impv. ἆρον 19:15, in view of the attendant cry σταύρωσον, is prob. to be understood as hoist (him), namely on a cross. Lift/draw up skiff Ac 27:17; abs. w. obj. understood weigh anchor vs. 13. In extended sense: of vocal sounds, ἦραν φωνήν (the lepers) raised their voice Lk 17:13. In imagery keep in suspense J 10:24.

—2. ‘move by lifting/taking from one position to another’, take away, remove, carry off Mt 21:21; 24:39; Lk 6:29; J 2:16; 19:31, 38; expel 1 Cor 5:2. Cp. 6:15. In imagery of sin taken away by Christ J 1:29; 1 J 3:5. In some of the Johannine passages there is probability of wordplay, with αἴρω functioning in combinations of upwardness, removal, or elimination. This seems to be the case esp. J 11:48; 15:2.
